topics: Introduction & relevance of biomedical signal processing. Biomedical signals - ECG
Aliasing-Sampling theorem. Elementary signals. Classification of discrete signals - Energy and Power signals - periodic and aperiodic

even and odd signals. Discrete time systems - Properties of discrete systems. LTI system-Impulse Response of a Linear Time-Invariant System -convolution-correlation - difference equation representation of discrete systems
Fourier Analysis, Fourier Analysis for Continuous Time Processes-Synthesis of an ECG signal using pure sinusoid. Frequency Domain Analysis of discrete time signals, Discrete Fourier series
Discrete Time Fourier Transform- Discrete Fourier Transform-properties
Fast Fourier Transform-FFT algorithms (Radix 2)- decimation in
Spectrum analysis of bio signals - Introduction to parametric & non parametric methods. Z transform - ROC
initial value theorem-rational Z transform-poles &zeros-LTI system function - inverse Z transform-long division-partial fraction expansion method-residue method-convolution method
types of digital filters. Comparison with analog filters. FIR filter design using Fourier series - use of window functions

IIR Digital Filters - Analog filter approximations -Butterworth approximation - Frequency transformation techniques - impulse invariant transformation - Matched Z transform technique- bilinear transformation Suggestion on Project Topics Students are asked to form groups of maximum five members. Each group should identify a project on the areas specified below and choose suitable data base. Develop a methodology to implement the project. Develop the algorithm and code for the project Areas of Projects Topics
Implementation of time domain Filters & Frequency domain filters for removal of high frequency and low frequency and periodic artefacts  Event detection in biomedical signals - ECG
P wave and T wave detection
dicrotic notch detection - Heart sound detection- EEG rhythm detection  Frequency Domain characterisation of Biomedical signal
Power spectrum density estimation of biomedical signals  Wave shape and Waveform Complexity analysis
